title: Samosa

seo.title: How to Make Samosa — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Samosa. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Samosa — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Samosas are crispy, savory pastries filled with spiced potatoes and peas, a quintessential Pakistani snack enjoyed across all regions. These triangular delights are deep-fried to golden perfection, offering a burst of flavor and texture in every bite. Perfect for tea time, Iftar, or as a delightful appetizer for any gathering, they are a true taste of home.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine flour, salt, and carom seeds. Add melted ghee or oil and rub it into the flour until it resembles breadcrumbs. Gradually add water and knead into a firm, smooth dough. Cover and let it rest for 20 minutes.

instructions.title: Cook the Filling Base
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent. Stir in ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Spice it Up
instructions.text: Add coriander powder, red chili powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly to prevent burning and to toast the spices.

instructions.title: Combine and Mash
instructions.text: Add the boiled and mashed potatoes and green peas to the pan. Mix well, ensuring the spices are evenly distributed. Cook for 5-7 minutes, gently mashing any large potato chunks to create a cohesive filling.

instructions.title: Finish the Filling
instructions.text: Stir in garam masala, amchur powder, and fresh coriander. Mix thoroughly and remove from heat. Let the filling cool completely before proceeding.

instructions.title: Shape the Samosas
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into equal small balls. Roll each ball into an oval shape, then cut it in half. Take one half, moisten the straight edge with water, and form a cone shape. Fill the cone with the cooled potato mixture, then seal the edges firmly, creating a triangular samosa. Repeat for all samosas.

instructions.title: Deep Fry Preparation
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil in a deep karahi or pot over medium heat. To test if the oil is hot enough, drop a small piece of dough into it; it should sizzle and rise immediately.

instructions.title: Fry Until Golden
instructions.text: Carefully place a few samosas into the hot oil, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Fry the samosas, turning occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides. This usually takes 7-10 minutes per batch.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Remove the fried samosas with a slotted spoon and place them on paper towels to drain any excess oil. Serve the hot, crispy samosas immediately with your favorite chutney, such as mint chutney or tamarind chutney.

tips: For a perfectly crispy samosa crust, ensure your dough is firm and rested. Adding a little ghee to the flour helps achieve that flaky, tender texture, a hallmark of authentic Pakistani samosas.

Always let your filling cool completely before filling the samosas. A hot filling can make the dough soggy and difficult to seal, leading to samosas bursting during frying. Cold filling also prevents steam buildup.

Fry samosas on medium-low heat. This allows the dough to cook through and become crispy without burning the outside, ensuring a uniform golden color and thoroughly cooked crust. High heat will brown them too quickly.
article: Samosa is a beloved Pakistani Appetizers dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
